narrative:
The Zebras are poised for an explosive offensive outing, headlined by Pete Crow-Armstrong and Yordan Alvarez. Crow-Armstrong faces a massive efficiency spike against Jake Irvin, bolstered by a rare confluence of favorable platoon splits, extreme SP skill advantage, and park-factor wind support that drives his projected score to a team-high 119.35. Alvarez remains an elite anchor in the two-hole against Jack Kochanowicz; with a projected 30% SP skill advantage and a favorable platoon matchup, he is the primary engine for our power and RBI production today. 

We must remain cautious with our platoon-sensitive assets, as the "Wind In" conditions are tempering the outlook for several starters, most notably Kyle Schwarber and Carson Benge. Schwarber’s projected output takes a significant hit from a negative L/L platoon matchup, requiring careful monitoring to ensure his xwOBA upside outweighs the environment. Meanwhile, Trea Turner and Pete Crow-Armstrong’s historical "BvP Elite" status provides a vital buffer against potential volatility, but the overall reliance on assumed lineup spots—specifically in the bottom third of the order—necessitates a final check before first pitch to ensure we aren’t sacrificing Roto efficiency on sub-optimal volume.
